Financial Support We believe that talented students from a variety of backgrounds should have the opportunity to study at Rose Bruford College and our teams are on hand to support you.
requirements: Our admissions team can help to explain more about this if you are unsure.

US Federal Loans If you are a US citizen and would like to apply for a US Federal Loans, please note that Rose Bruford College is a recognised institution and you are eligible to apply for a US Federal Loan to fund your studies with us.
For further information on this, please contact the Admissions team at [email protected].

items.title: Fees & funding
items.funding: Our most talented students have all their fees covered by scholarships and almost half of RCM students receive some financial support from us.
requirements: Please check the different types below, expanding each section for details on eligibility and how to apply.

items.summary: If you are planning to study as an undergraduate or postgraduate student at the Royal College of Music, you should consider how you will finance your studies.
items.sourceName: Royal College of Music via UCAS provider directory

items.title: RCM scholarships and awards
items.funding: Two scholarships to International students (one undergraduate, one postgraduate), which are worth the cost of full tuition fees plus a £5000 per annum stipend for living expenses.
requirements: Please check the different types below, expanding each section for details on eligibility and how to apply.

How to be considered Eligibility for scholarships is based on merit, which is determined by performance at audition (or by submission of video) or the strength of portfolio and interview for composers.
Students from households with an income of £25,000 or lower will be eligible for a bursary of £3,000 for each year of the BMus course.

items.title: Scholarships
items.funding: Funding signal found, but a clean stipend summary still needs review.
requirements: Academic Excellence Scholarship You may be eligible for the Academic Excellence Scholarship during your programme if you meet the academic criteria described below.

❓ How do I qualify for the Choose Dalarna First Scholarship, and do I need to apply separately?To qualify, you must: Rank any campus-based Dalarna University programme as your first choice when applying on UniversityAdmissions.se.
Even if you are admitted to a different DU programme than your first choice, you may still receive the scholarship as long as any eligible DU programme was your top choice.
items.eligibility: Research Publications (DiVA) Course Syllabi Programme Syllabi Literature lists Documents Study at Dalarna University Research at DU Library About DU Home Study at Dalarna University Research Library About Dalarna University Student web Study at Dalarna University Programmes, courses and course packages New students Education system Outbound Exchange Studies and Traineeships International students: application and admission information Study by distance Computer Skills and Computer Capacity International students: application and admission information Deferment of studies How and when to apply Application: Later Stage of a Programme Residence Permits Entry requirements Incoming exchange students: nomination and application process Application fees and tuition fees Scholarships Non-Completion of Studies Waiting list Scholarships Scholarships You are guaranteed a tuition scholarship when you apply to a Dalarna University master’s programme as your first choice and are admitted.
items.summary: You are guaranteed a tuition scholarship when you apply to a Dalarna University master’s programme as your first choice and are admitted. You can also earn additional scholarships based on your academic results during your studies.

I was also the first in my family to earn a degree, with no friends who shared my interests or background in tech.

items.title: University scholarships and bursaries
items.funding: As the cost-of-living continues to increase, we understand how important it is to get financial support.
requirements: Eligible students may receive between £500 and £2,000 in cash per academic year to help with essential costs, enabling them to focus on their studies and university experience.
Students with caring responsibilities, those eligible for free school meals, refugees, Gypsies, Travellers, Roma, Showmen and Boaters (GTRSB).
You must be a 'Home', or ‘Overseas Island’ student on an eligible course The Government classes all students as ‘Home’ or ‘International’.
items.summary: Scholarships and bursaries are free financial awards that you don't need to pay back.
